title: Ag Nanocrystals: 1. Effect of Ligands on Plasmonic Properties

oleylamine_coated_nanocrystals.method: hot-injection with AgNO3 and oleylamine into hot dodecanediol solution

dodecylamine_coated_nanocrystals.description: Similar to oleylamine but with shorter alkyl chain (C12 vs C18)

dodecanethiol_coated_nanocrystals.method: ligand exchange from preformed oleylamine-coated NCs
dodecanethiol_coated_nanocrystals.ligand_exchange_procedure: 20 microliters dodecanethiol per 10 mg Ag NCs, 1 hour stirring

hexadecanethiol_coated_nanocrystals.method: ligand exchange procedure similar to dodecanethiol

ligand_exchange_analysis.test_sample: 6 nm oleylamine-coated Ag NCs exchanged to dodecanethiol
ligand_exchange_analysis.conclusion: At least 80% of amine-terminated ligands successfully exchanged for thiol compounds

oleylamine_coated_nanocrystals.spr_trend: Blue-shift (shift to smaller wavelengths and higher energies) with increasing NC size
oleylamine_coated_nanocrystals.mechanism: Plasmonic core/shell theory - electron conductivity lower in outer Ag layer bound to coating agents

dodecylamine_coated_nanocrystals.spr_trend: Same size-dependent blue-shift pattern as oleylamine
dodecylamine_coated_nanocrystals.comparison: SPR band position shows no significant shift with alkyl chain length change (C12 vs C18)

dodecanethiol_vs_hexadecanethiol.observation: Significant shift based on alkyl chain length
dodecanethiol_vs_hexadecanethiol.mechanism: Stronger chemical bond between thiol and metallic surface makes SPR sensitive to ligand chain length variations

amine_vs_thiol_coated.observation: Alkanethiol (Cx-SH) coated NCs show SPR bands at much lower energies than alkylamine (Cx-NH2) coated NCs
amine_vs_thiol_coated.mechanism: Thiol head groups bond more strongly than amine-terminated ligands to metallic surfaces

key_findings: All NC types regardless of size arranged in 2D hexagonal closed-packed arrays
Well-defined SPR bands characteristic of low size distribution
Absorption spectra show normalized optical responses
No aggregation observed before or after ligand exchange

electron_microscopy_observations.nanocrystal_arrangement: 2D hexagonal closed-packed arrays characteristic of low size distribution (<10%)
electron_microscopy_observations.high_resolution_tem: All NCs confirmed as icosahedral structures
electron_microscopy_observations.literature_context: Same icosahedral crystals reported by Peng et al. for Ag NCs

comparative_analysis.synthesis_efficiency: New platform for size-controllable synthesis of Ag NCs without size-focusing process

ligand_type_effects.amine_ligands: SPR position largely independent of alkyl chain length
ligand_type_effects.thiol_ligands: SPR position significantly dependent on alkyl chain length
ligand_type_effects.explanation: Bond strength differences affect sensitivity to ligand structural variations

typical_6nm_synthesis.silver_precursor: 0.1 g AgNO3 in 5 mL DCB mixed with 1 mL oleylamine
typical_6nm_synthesis.cooling_method: Natural cooling to room temperature after 180 seconds

key_conclusions: Size-controllable Ag NC synthesis (2.2-12.9 nm) achievable without post-synthesis size segregation
Ligand type significantly affects SPR properties for given NC size
Thiol-stabilized NCs show chain-length dependent SPR shifts; amine-stabilized do not
Strong thiol-metal bonds create enhanced sensitivity to ligand structural variations
Successful ligand exchange from amines to thiols (>80% efficiency) enables post-synthesis modification of NC properties
